title: Conditionals: choose the correct verb form
lesson: Conditionals link a condition to its result. The first conditional — if + present simple, will + base verb — talks about realistic future events ('If it rains, we will stay'). The second — if + past, would + base verb — imagines unreal or unlikely situations ('If I were rich…').

items.text: If it {1} tomorrow, we will stay home.
items.explanation: First conditional: if + present simple, will + base verb. 'If it rains…'.

items.text: If I {1} rich, I would travel the world.
items.explanation: Second conditional uses the past form ('were' for all persons) for an unreal present: 'If I were rich…'.

items.text: If you {1} hard, you {2} the exam.
items.explanation: First conditional: if + present simple (study), will + base verb (will pass) for a realistic future event.
Zero conditional for general truths: present simple in both clauses.

items.text: If they {1} us, we {2} the project on time.
items.explanation: Third conditional: if + past perfect (had helped), would have + past participle (would have finished) — an unreal past.

items.text: If she {1} earlier, she {2} the train and {3} home by now.

blanks.hint: present result of a past condition

items.explanation: Third conditional for the past action (had left / would have caught), but 'would be' — not 'would have been' — for a present result: she would be home by now.
Zero conditional for scientific facts: present simple in both clauses (heat … boils).

items.text: If we {1} now, we {2} the start of the film.
items.explanation: First conditional: if + present simple (leave), will/won't + base verb (won't miss) for a realistic future.

items.text: If I {1} you, I {2} the job.
items.explanation: Second conditional for advice in an unreal situation: 'If I were you, I would accept…' ('were' for all persons).

items.text: If she {1} more time, she {2} a longer trip.
items.explanation: Second conditional for an imagined present situation: if + past simple (had), would + base verb (would take).

items.text: If we {1} the map, we {2} lost.
items.explanation: Third conditional for an unreal past: if + past perfect (had brought), would(n't) have + past participle (wouldn't have got).

items.text: Unless you {1} now, you will miss your bus.
items.explanation: First conditional: 'unless' means 'if … not', so it still takes the present simple ('hurry') in the condition clause, with 'will' in the result.

items.text: As long as you {1} quiet, the baby {2} asleep.
items.explanation: First conditional with 'as long as' (a condition): present simple in the condition clause (stay), will + base verb in the result (will stay).

items.text: If I {1} about the meeting, I {2} you, but nobody told me.
items.explanation: Third conditional for an unreal past: if + past perfect (had known), would have + past participle (would have called) — the speaker didn't know, so didn't call.
